name: Marvin (AI Functions and Type-Safe Extraction)

auth.notes: LLM provider API key (OpenAI by default)

performance.notes: LLM-dependent; structured output adds minimal overhead over base LLM call

agent_readiness.notes: AI functions with Pydantic types. @marvin.fn decorator makes any function AI-powered. marvin.extract(text, target=list[str]). marvin.classify(text, labels=['pos', 'neg']). marvin.cast(text, target=MyModel). marvin.generate(n=5, instructions='...'). AI images via marvin.paint(). Feels like writing normal Python — LLM complexity hidden. Built by Prefect team. Compatible with any OpenAI-compatible API. v2 is complete rewrite. Similar to instructor but more magic/opinionated.

gotchas: Magic function behavior can be opaque for debugging
v2 API is completely different from v1
Less control than instructor for complex extraction
best_when: Rapid prototyping of AI-powered Python functions
Text extraction, classification, and transformation in data pipelines
Prefect workflow users wanting LLM integration
Teams wanting minimal boilerplate for structured LLM output
avoid_when: Need explicit control over prompts (instructor more transparent)
summary: Marvin wraps LLMs in pythonic AI functions. @marvin.fn turns any function AI-powered. extract(), classify(), cast() for structured text operations. Minimal boilerplate — LLM complexity hidden behind normal Python. Built by Prefect team. Best for rapid prototyping and data pipelines needing quick AI-powered extraction and classification. Instructor offers more control for production structured output; Outlines for local model guarantees.
